text.title: Docker Compose (CI/CD) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Docker Compose (CI/CD) methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: CI override MUST remove all ports: bindings. Tests reach services on the compose network only.
rule.rationale: CI override MUST remove all ports: bindings. Tests reach services on the compose network only.

rule.statement: Pipeline uses 'docker compose up -d --wait' AND every service has a healthcheck. depends_on with condition: service_healthy required for gated services.
rule.rationale: Pipeline uses 'docker compose up -d --wait' AND every service has a healthcheck. depends_on with condition: service_healthy required for gated services.

rule.statement: Pipeline ends with 'docker compose down -v --remove-orphans' inside an 'if: always()' step. Without it stale volumes break the next run.
rule.rationale: Pipeline ends with 'docker compose down -v --remove-orphans' inside an 'if: always()' step. Without it stale volumes break the next run.

rule.statement: CI secrets come from CI env vars (secrets: in GHA). .env files in CI are forbidden — they leak via cache.
rule.rationale: CI secrets come from CI env vars (secrets: in GHA). .env files in CI are forbidden — they leak via cache.

rule.statement: Compose override file lists a # owner: line in the header. Forbidden to omit.
rule.rationale: Compose override file lists a # owner: line in the header. Forbidden to omit.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-docker-compose.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
